perturbation.original_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. RETURN OF INTELLECTUAL ASSETS All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Intellectual Assets to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.explanation: The switch from 'Confidential Information' to 'Intellectual Assets' implies a potentially broader or different scope, but without definition. Section 1.2 defines what "Confidential Information" is and now it is being called "Intellectual Assets". This contradicts the previously given definition as it remains unclear whether 'Intellectual Assets' encompasses everything in 1.2 or only certain items.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 “Confidential Information” shall mean all information provided by Disclosing Party with respect to the Invention regardless of whether it is written, oral, audio tapes, video tapes, computer disc s, machines, prototypes, designs, specifications, articles of manufacture, drawings, huma n or machine readable documents. Confidential Information shall also include all information related to the Invention provided by Disclosing Pa rty to Receiving Party prior to the signing Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 of this agreement. Confidential Information shall not include any information in the public domain at the time of the disclosure, or subsequently comes within the public domain without fault of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Intellectual Assets' creates a strong in-text contradiction. Section 1.2 explicitly defines 'Confidential Information,' and the modified text in Section 3 replaces this term with 'Intellectual Assets' without providing a definition. This contradicts Section 1.2 because it is now unclear whether 'Intellectual Assets' encompasses the same information as defined in 1.2, leading to ambiguity and potential disputes regarding the scope of the return obligation. The Perturbation Explanation also confirms this by stating that this change 'implies a potentially broader or different scope, but without definition' and it is 'unclear whether 'Intellectual Assets' encompasses everything in 1.2 or only certain items.'

perturbation.explanation: Changing the titles from 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party' to 'Vendor' and 'Recipient' introduces new terms without defining them. The document starts by explicitly naming the parties as "Disclosing Party" and "Receiving Party". This creates in-text contradiction, particularly in enforcement, as it is not clear whether the Vendor/Recipient have the same obligations as the originally defined Disclosing/Receiving party. Now the contract will have to be interpreted to figure out how to call each party in question.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement, made this ____ day of ___________, 2009, between _________________________, (hereinafter “Disclosing Party”), and _________________________(hereinafter “Receiving Party”). BACKGROUND The Disclosing Party and Receiving Party wish to discuss and exchange certain items and information (the “Inven tion”) which the parties hereto consider highly confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party' with 'Vendor' and 'Recipient'. The contradicted text defines the parties involved in the agreement as 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party'. This introduces misaligned terminology as per the definitions provided and creates a direct contradiction because the contract now uses different terms to refer to the parties without clarification, making it unclear whether 'Vendor' and 'Recipient' have the same obligations as 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party'.

perturbation.original_text: 9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Agreement shall be binding upon either party unless reduced in writing and signed by them.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. COMPLETE UNDERSTANDING This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Understanding shall be binding upon either party unless reduced in writing and signed by them.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT' to 'COMPLETE UNDERSTANDING', creates an in-text contradiction about the context of what is being reffered, making it uncertain in future enforcement, and if the same rules applied for the previous entire agreement will remain the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 1 CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement, made this ____ day of ___________, 2009, between _________________________, (hereinafter “Disclosing Party”), and _________________________(hereinafter “Receiving Party”). BACKGROUND The Disclosing Party and Receiving Party wish to discuss and exchange certain items and information (the “Inven tion”) which the parties hereto consider highly confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT,' while the changed text uses 'COMPLETE UNDERSTANDING.' The contradiction arises because the change creates uncertainty about the context being referred to. While both phrases aim to encapsulate the comprehensive nature of the agreement, the Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement references 'This Agreement' and given that the terminology used to define that agreement was modified, the contradiction is triggered. The modification raises doubts about whether the same rules and previous applications of the 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT' term apply to the new 'COMPLETE UNDERSTANDING' term, leading to potential confusion.

perturbation.original_text: 6. NO LICENSE Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. NO RIGHT Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Sensitive Data, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'NO LICENSE' to 'NO RIGHT', and 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data', creates an in-text contradiction about the context of what is being reffered, since the term wasn't previous mention, making it uncertain what sensitive data is being reffered and creating a conflict between the NO LICENSE section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 “Confidential Information” shall mean all information provided by Disclosing Party with respect to the Invention regardless of whether it is written, oral, audio tapes, video tapes, computer disc s, machines, prototypes, designs, specifications, articles of manufacture, drawings, huma n or machine readable documents. Confidential Information shall also include all information related to the Invention provided by Disclosing Pa rty to Receiving Party prior to the signing Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 of this agreement. Confidential Information shall not include any information in the public domain at the time of the disclosure, or subsequently comes within the public domain without fault of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' in the Changed Text creates an in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text defines 'Confidential Information' explicitly, whereas 'Sensitive Data' is not defined anywhere in the provided context. This misalignment in terminology, as per the provided definition, introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, leading to a conflict and making it unclear what the 'NO RIGHT' clause applies to, which creates a strong in-text contradiction.
